S1: Measurements: Physical quantities, SI units, and using measuring instruments.
States of Matter: Particle theory and physical properties.
Introduction to Forces: Types of forces, mass, and weight.
Thermometry: Temperature scales and measuring instruments.
Heat Transfer: Conduction, convection, and radiation.
Rectilinear Propagation of Light: Shadows, eclipses, and pinhole cameras.
Reflection of Light: Plane surfaces and laws of reflection.
Introduction to Electricity & Magnetism: Basic circuits, conductors, insulators, and magnetic fields.
S2: Turning Effect of Forces: Moments, center of gravity, and equilibrium.
Machines: Levers, pulleys, and mechanical advantage.
Work, Energy, and Power: Definitions, calculations, and the principle of conservation of energy.
Pressure: Hydrostatic pressure, atmospheric pressure, and hydraulic systems.
Properties of Matter: Elasticity, surface tension, and capillarity.
Reflection at Curved Surfaces: Concave and convex mirrors, ray diagrams.
Waves: Progressive and stationary waves, wave properties.
Sound Waves: Production, transmission, and characteristics (pitch, loudness).
Electricity & Magnetism: Magnetic effect of electric current, electromagnets, and electric current basics.
S3: Electrostatics: Production of electric charges, electric fields, and capacitors.
Current Electricity: Ohm's law, resistors in series and parallel, and electromotive force (EMF).
Magnetism & Electromagnetism: Earth's magnetic field and electromagnetic induction (Faraday's and Lenz's laws).
Thermal Physics: Specific heat capacity, expansion of solids/liquids, and gas laws.
Refraction of Light: Laws of refraction, refractive index, and total internal reflection.
Lenses: Thin converging and diverging lenses, and optical instruments (e.g., cameras, projectors).
S4: Electronics: Thermionic emission, diodes, and basic logic gates.
Atomic Physics: Structure of the atom, radioactivity, and half-life.
Nuclear Physics: Fission, fusion, and uses of nuclear energy.
Electromagnetic Spectrum: Characteristics and uses of various waves (radio, X-rays, gamma).
Electronics Applications: Semi-conductors and simple electronic devices.
Practical Physics: UNEB standard experiments (e.g., pendulum, electricity circuits, and optics benches)

Report_Format: Hypothesis: Statement linking 1 Independent to 1 Dependent variable
Presentation of Data: Table, Graph, Calculation of Slope
Conclusion: Must qualify or disqualify the hypothesis

Key_Notes.Hypothesis_Definition: A prediction to be tested. Shows relationship between variables.
